user_task: Run a long ingestion job (>1 min) in the background. Async ingestion for jobs that take > 1 min: kick off the job, get an immediate acknowledgment, keep working, and observe terminal status / query the index later.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, INGEST bucket — 'Run a long ingestion job (>1 min) in the background'. Seed queries (paraphrased) from the 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, INGEST -> async/background ingestion: 'Start a big ingest job on these N PDFs — I'll come back to check on it.'; 'Kick this off in the background; don't make me wait.'; 'I have a lot of docs — start ingesting and let me poll for status.'

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — a long-running NRL ingest job is moved to the BACKGROUND so the agent regains control immediately. Pass = (1) the skill does NOT block on full completion; it backgrounds the job and an initial acknowledgment (a started/queued signal, e.g. a captured PID or the first stream event / job_created) is surfaced; (2) the agent can perform another action while the job runs (proven non-blocking); (3) progress is observable (per-file completion events / a progress tail from the stream); (4) terminal status (succeeded / failed / partial) is detectable from the same channel and the index is queryable afterward. Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: two-phase. ACKNOWLEDGMENT is FAST: the started/queued ack lands <= 5s after submission (this is the gated SLA for the row). The FULL job is SLOW (the whole point: > 1 min) and is NOT gated on wall-clock here — the test SHAPE (background + quick ack + observable terminal status) is under test, not literal duration on this small fixture corpus.
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'start a big ingest in the background, I'll check on it later' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must reach `retriever ingest` for the actual work and BACKGROUND it (shell-level: `nohup ... &` / a background task wrapping `retriever ingest`, tailing its log/quiet summary), OR drive service mode (`retriever service start` then `retriever service ingest --sse` / `--no-sse --poll-interval`) which exposes the job-scoped event stream. Must NOT invent a `--async` flag or a `task_id` REST poll on the plain CLI — neither exists in 26.05.

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <paths...>` auto-detects PDF from the extension. There is NO --input-type flag.

two_backgrounding_paths.shell_level: The plain `retriever ingest <dir>` command is SYNCHRONOUS (it blocks until the LanceDB write completes and prints one summary line: `Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.`). There is no `--async`/`--background`/`--detach` flag and no `--run-mode service` on the plain ingest command (run-mode is `inprocess|batch` only). Backgrounding is therefore done at the ORCHESTRATION layer: `nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo $!` returns the PID as the immediate ack (< 5s); progress is observed by tailing ingest.log (with --no-quiet the CLI emits per-stage / per-file progress); terminal status = process exit code + the final `Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s)` summary line in the log; the index is then queryable with `retriever query`. This path requires NO running server.
two_backgrounding_paths.service_mode: The `service` sub-app DOES expose a streaming/async surface built on `ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream()` (src/nemo_retriever/service/service_ingestor.py). Flow: `retriever service start` boots a FastAPI server; `retriever service ingest <files> --sse` opens a server-side job aggregate via `POST /v1/ingest/job` (returns an opaque `job_id`, sized to len(files)), uploads each doc, and subscribes to the per-job SSE stream `GET /v1/ingest/job/{job_id}/events`. Events: `job_created` (carries job_id + expected_documents) -> `job_started` -> `upload_complete` / `document_complete` (per file) -> `job_progress` (completed/failed counts) -> terminal `job_finalized` | `job_partial` | `job_failed`. With `--no-sse` the client bulk-POLLs `POST /v1/ingest/status/batch` every `--poll-interval` seconds (default 2.0s) and the aggregate is fetchable at `GET /v1/ingest/job/{job_id}`. The first event (`job_created`) is the quick ack. This is the closest thing to native async; it still requires a running server and is NOT a plain-CLI task_id call.

environment.no_native_async_api: NRL 26.05 has NO public task_id + REST-poll API on the plain `retriever ingest` CLI. The 'submit -> get task_id -> GET /status until done' experience is a SKILL-LAYER construct built on top of either shell backgrounding (PID + log tail of `retriever ingest`) or the service-mode SSE/poll API (`job_id` + `/v1/ingest/job/{job_id}/events`). Any expected output that names a `task_id` REST endpoint on the plain CLI, or an `--async`/`--background` flag on `retriever ingest`, is WRONG and must fail the case.
environment.fixture_note: The per-case corpus is a small 5-PDF folder (multimodal_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf, test-page-form.pdf, embedded_table.pdf). On this small corpus the real wall-clock is well under a minute; a genuine > 1 min job needs a larger corpus (e.g. the financebench 10-K set under /raid/retriever-sdg-v3/test-data/financebench/). The SHAPE — background submission + <=5s ack + observable terminal status + post-completion query — is what's under test, not literal duration.
environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int.

cases.title: Baseline: kick off a folder ingest in the background, get an immediate ack (control returns)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the INGEST async-task CORE at its simplest: the skill backgrounds `retriever ingest` and returns an immediate acknowledgment (a captured PID / job-started signal) instead of blocking on full completion. Rung 1 only asserts the job is launched in the background and an ack lands quickly; it does not yet require the agent to do other work, observe progress, or surface a terminal state. This is the floor.
cases.prompt: Start a big ingest job on the PDFs in data/ for me — I'll come back to check on it later. Kick it off in the background and just confirm it started; don't make me wait for it to finish.
expected_commands: nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo "started pid $!"

expected_output.ack: an immediate started/launched acknowledgment within ~5s: a backgrounded process PID (from `echo $!`) or equivalent 'job started' confirmation; the agent's turn is NOT blocked on the full ingest
expected_output.backgrounding: shell-level backgrounding (nohup ... & / background task) wrapping the SYNCHRONOUS `retriever ingest data/`; there is no --async/--background flag on the CLI
expected_output.eventual_summary: ingest.log will EVENTUALLY contain `Ingested 5 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.` once the job finishes (NOT awaited at rung 1)

pass_when: the skill runs `retriever ingest data/` over the 5-PDF folder but BACKGROUNDS it (nohup/&/background task), so control returns to the agent
an immediate acknowledgment (PID or 'started' signal) is surfaced <= 5s after submission
the agent does NOT block its turn waiting for the full ingest to complete
no invented `--async`/`--background` flag and no fictional `task_id` REST poll on the plain CLI
cases.known_caveat: There is no native background/async flag on `retriever ingest`; the backgrounding is shell-level (nohup ... &). The ack is the captured PID, not a server-issued task_id.

cases.title: Non-blocking: the agent performs another action while the ingest runs
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'agent stays responsive' clause. Adds over rung 1 exactly one dimension: after backgrounding the job and getting the ack, the agent must DO SOMETHING ELSE while the ingest is still running (e.g. list the input files / report how many docs were submitted / inspect the log so far) — proving the background launch is genuinely non-blocking, not just a fast synchronous run. New dimension: a concurrent agent action overlapping the live job.
cases.prompt: I have a lot of docs in data/ — start ingesting them in the background, and while that's running, tell me how many PDFs you actually submitted and which files they are. Don't wait for the ingest to finish before answering.
expected_commands: nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo "started pid $!"

expected_output.ack: background launch ack (PID) <= 5s, same as rung 1
expected_output.concurrent_action: while the ingest process is alive, the agent answers a second question — there are 5 PDFs submitted: multimodal_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf, test-page-form.pdf, embedded_table.pdf
expected_output.non_blocking_proof: the second answer is produced BEFORE the ingest summary line appears (the agent did not stall on `retriever ingest`)

pass_when: ingest of data/ is backgrounded with a quick ack (as rung 1)
the agent performs and completes a SEPARATE action (counts/lists the 5 submitted PDFs) while the ingest is still in flight
the concurrent answer comes back without waiting for the full ingest to complete (non-blocking proven)
report correctly identifies 5 PDFs by name

cases.title: Progress observable: per-file / progress events can be tailed from the running job
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'progress is observable' clause. Adds over rung 2: it is no longer enough to launch-and-forget — the skill must expose a WAY TO OBSERVE PROGRESS of the live job. Either tail the `--no-quiet` ingest.log for per-stage/per-file lines (shell path), OR drive service mode (`retriever service ingest --sse`) and read the per-file `document_complete` / `job_progress` events off the SSE stream built on ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream(). New dimension: a polling/progress read of an in-flight job.
cases.prompt: Kick off the ingest of data/ in the background, then let me poll its status — show me progress as files get processed so I can see it's moving, not just that it started.
expected_commands: nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo "started pid $!"
tail -n +1 -f ingest.log   # (or periodic `tail -n 20 ingest.log`) to read progress
# service-mode alternative: $RETRIEVER service start &  then  $RETRIEVER service ingest data/*.pdf --sse  (reads job_created/job_progress/document_complete events)

expected_output.ack: quick background ack (PID, or `job_created` event carrying job_id in service mode) <= 5s
expected_output.progress_observable: the skill surfaces a progress read of the IN-FLIGHT job: tailing ingest.log shows per-stage/per-file progress lines (shell path), or the SSE stream emits `job_progress` (completed/failed counts) and `document_complete` per file (service path)
expected_output.mechanism_note: polling/progress here is a SKILL-LAYER construct over the stream (log tail OR aingest_stream() SSE events) — NOT a native task_id status REST call on the plain CLI

pass_when: ingest is backgrounded with a quick ack
the skill provides a concrete progress-observation mechanism on the live job: tail of the --no-quiet ingest.log, OR service-mode SSE `job_progress`/`document_complete` events
progress reflects files moving (a count climbing toward 5, or per-file completion lines) — not merely the start signal
no fictional native `task_id` poll endpoint is used on the plain `retriever ingest` CLI
cases.known_caveat: Progress observability is built at the skill layer: either tailing the `--no-quiet` log of the backgrounded `retriever ingest`, or consuming the service-mode SSE events (job_progress / document_complete) which are the surface of ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream(). The plain CLI has no status endpoint.

cases.title: Terminal status detectable + index queryable after the job finishes
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'terminal status observable + queryable after' clause. Adds over rung 3: progress was visible mid-flight; now the skill must detect the TERMINAL state (succeeded / failed / partial) from the same channel AND prove the index is usable afterward with a real query. Shell path: process exit code 0 + the final `Ingested 5 file(s) -> M row(s)` summary in the log = success; then `retriever query` returns a grounded hit. Service path: terminal `job_finalized` (vs `job_partial`/`job_failed`) event. New dimension: terminal-status detection + a post-completion retrieval.
cases.prompt: Run the ingest of data/ in the background and let me know when it's done and whether it succeeded. Once it's finished, prove the index works: who owns the woods in the Robert Frost poem?
expected_commands: nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo "started pid $!"
wait $!; echo "exit=$?"   # detect terminal status (exit 0 + summary line in ingest.log)

expected_output.terminal_status: SUCCEEDED is detectable: backgrounded process exits 0 AND ingest.log ends with `Ingested 5 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.` (service-mode equivalent: a `job_finalized` terminal event, vs `job_partial`/`job_failed`)
expected_output.post_completion_query: after terminal success, `retriever query` returns a grounded hit; the owner's 'house is in the village' (he will not see the speaker stopping) — cite woods_frost.pdf p1

pass_when: ingest backgrounded with quick ack, then run to completion
terminal status SUCCEEDED is surfaced from the same channel: exit code 0 + final `Ingested 5 file(s) -> M row(s)` summary line (or a `job_finalized` SSE event in service mode)
a post-completion `retriever query` returns >= 1 grounded hit citing woods_frost.pdf p1 (owner's 'house is in the village')
the distinction succeeded/failed/partial is observable, not assumed

cases.title: Acceptance: long job backgrounded, <=5s ack, agent stays responsive, terminal status surfaced, post-completion query works
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the INGEST async-task row. Composes ALL prior rungs into one flow: (1) a long ingest is BACKGROUNDED with an acknowledgment <= 5s, (2) the agent stays RESPONSIVE (does another action mid-flight), (3) PROGRESS is observable, (4) TERMINAL status (succeeded/failed/partial) is surfaced, and (5) a POST-COMPLETION query against the freshly built index returns a grounded answer with a citation. Carries the explicit note that the polling/task_id experience is a SKILL-LAYER construct over the stream (log tail or aingest_stream() SSE), NOT a native 26.05 API. This is the gate the others build up to.
cases.prompt: I have a big batch of PDFs in data/ that'll take a while to ingest. Start the job in the background and confirm within a few seconds that it's running. While it runs, tell me how many docs are in the batch. Then poll it so I can see progress, let me know when it finishes and whether it fully succeeded, and finally prove the index is live: what value did James have in 2019 (pull it from the table)?
expected_commands: nohup $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --use-table-structure --no-quiet > ingest.log 2>&1 & echo "started pid $!"
ls data/*.pdf | wc -l            # responsive: report 5 docs while job runs
tail -n 20 ingest.log            # observe progress on the in-flight job
wait $!; echo "exit=$?"          # terminal status: exit 0 + summary line

expected_output.ack: background launch confirmed with a PID within ~5s of submission (the gated fast-ack SLA)
expected_output.responsive: while the job runs the agent reports the batch size = 5 PDFs (multimodal_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf, test-page-form.pdf, embedded_table.pdf)
expected_output.progress_observable: a progress read of the live job (log tail showing per-file/per-stage lines; service-mode equivalent = job_progress/document_complete SSE events)
expected_output.terminal_status: SUCCEEDED surfaced: process exit 0 + ingest.log ends with `Ingested 5 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.` (or terminal `job_finalized` event)
expected_output.post_completion_query: James 2019 = 978, pulled from a content_type=table row; cite table_test.pdf p1
expected_output.skill_layer_note: polling/terminal-status here is a SKILL-LAYER construct over the stream (log tail of backgrounded `retriever ingest`, or ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream() SSE events) — NOT a native task_id REST API in 26.05

pass_when: long ingest of data/ is BACKGROUNDED and a started ack (PID) lands <= 5s after submission
the agent stays responsive: it reports the 5-doc batch size while the ingest is still in flight
progress is observable on the live job (log tail or SSE job_progress/document_complete)
terminal status SUCCEEDED is surfaced (exit 0 + final summary line, or job_finalized) — succeeded/failed/partial is distinguishable, not assumed
post-completion `retriever query --content-types text,table` returns James 2019 = 978 from a table row, citing table_test.pdf p1
NO invented `--async`/`--background` flag and NO fictional native `task_id` REST poll on the plain CLI; backgrounding/polling is the skill-layer construct over the stream
cases.known_caveat: ACCEPTANCE GATE CAVEAT: NRL 26.05 has no public task_id + REST-poll API on the plain `retriever ingest` CLI, and no `--async`/`--background`/`--detach`/`--run-mode service` flag on it (plain ingest run-mode is inprocess|batch only). A 'pass' means the skill backgrounds the job and surfaces a quick ack + observable progress/terminal status via the STREAM — shell-level (nohup ... & + --no-quiet log tail of `retriever ingest`) or service-mode SSE/poll (`retriever service start` then `retriever service ingest --sse`/`--no-sse --poll-interval`, exposing job_created->job_progress->job_finalized over ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream()). On this small 5-PDF corpus wall-clock is < 1 min; a genuine > 1 min job needs a larger corpus (e.g. financebench 10-Ks). The SHAPE is under test, not literal duration.
